title: stimoryne axillaris
intro: Stimoryne axillaris, also known as the dwarf or purple fiddleneck, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The medicinal properties of Stimoryne axillaris are primarily attributed to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Stimoryne axillaris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
